title: Density
summary: Defines conformal densities through equivariance under positive metric rescaling and relates equivariant and scale-dependent presentations.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines conformal densities through equivariance under positive metric rescaling and relates equivariant and scale-dependent presentations.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Conformal/Density.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For α a type, the weight is defined to be Pos Real maps to times α.

Rescaling a representative from s₁ to s₂. In explicit terms, for χ of type weight α, s₁, s₂ of type conf.scale, and f of type m maps to α, its defining expression is the function sending x to χ Conf.relative Factor s₁ s₂ x times f x.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{rescale}(χ,s₁,s₂,f)=x \longmapsto χ (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{relativeFactor} s₁ s₂ x) \cdot f x
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def rescale (χ : Weight α) (s₁ s₂ : Conf.Scale) (f : M → α) : M → α

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A density described by its representative in every scale. Its mathematical data are: val: Conf.Scale maps to M maps to α; compat: for every s₁ s₂ : Conf.Scale, val s₂ equals rescale Conf χ s₁ s₂ val s₁; instance: Coe Fun Density Conf : equals Conf χ fun maps to Conf.Scale maps to M maps to α where coe d : equals d.val @ simp theorem compat apply d : Density Conf : equals Conf χ s₁ s₂ : Conf.Scale x : M : d s₂ x equals χ Conf.relative Factor s₁ s₂ x times d s₁ x : equals by simpa rescale using congr Fun d.compat s₁ s₂ x.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{val}&\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{Scale} \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow α\\\operatorname{compat}&\colon \forall s₁ s₂ \colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{Scale}, \operatorname{val} s₂ = \operatorname{rescale} \operatorname{Conf} χ s₁ s₂ (\operatorname{val} s₁)\\\operatorname{instance}&\colon \operatorname{CoeFun} (\operatorname{Density} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) χ) (\operatorname{fun} _ => \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{Scale} \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow α) \operatorname{where} \operatorname{coe} d = d.\operatorname{val} @[\operatorname{simp}] \operatorname{theorem} \operatorname{compat\_apply} (d \colon \operatorname{Density} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) χ) (s₁ s₂ \colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{Scale}) (x \colon M) \colon d s₂ x = χ (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{relativeFactor} s₁ s₂ x) \cdot d s₁ x = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{simpa} [\operatorname{rescale}] \operatorname{using} \operatorname{congrFun} (d.\operatorname{compat} s₁ s₂) x\end{aligned}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The representative in a chosen scale. In explicit terms, for d of type density conf : equals conf χ, and s of type conf.scale, its defining expression is d s.
correspondence.leanType: def repr (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) (s : Conf.Scale) : M → α

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: An equivariant function on the metric bundle. Its mathematical data are: to Fun: Conf.Metric Bundle maps to α; equivariant: for every r : Pos Real u : Conf.Metric Bundle, to Fun Conf.dilate Bundle r u equals χ r times to Fun u; instance: Coe Fun Density Conf : equals Conf χ fun maps to Conf.Metric Bundle maps to α where coe d : equals d.to Fun @ simp theorem equivariant apply d : Density Conf : equals Conf χ r : Pos Real u : Conf.Metric Bundle : d Conf.dilate Bundle r u equals χ r times d u : equals d.equivariant r u @ ext theorem ext d₁ d₂ : Equivariant.Density Conf : equals Conf χ h : for every u : Conf.Metric Bundle, d₁.to Fun u equals d₂.to Fun u : d₁ equals d₂ : equals by cases d₁ with | mk f₁ h₁ maps to cases d₂ with | mk f₂ h₂ maps to have hf : f₁ equals f₂ : equals funext h cases hf have hh : h₁ equals h₂ : equals by apply Subsingleton.elim cases hh rfl.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{toFun}&\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{MetricBundle} \longrightarrow α\\\operatorname{equivariant}&\colon \forall (r \colon \operatorname{PosReal}) (u \colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{MetricBundle}), \operatorname{toFun} (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{dilateBundle} r u) = χ r \cdot \operatorname{toFun} u\\\operatorname{instance}&\colon \operatorname{CoeFun} (\operatorname{Density} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) χ) (\operatorname{fun} _ => \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{MetricBundle} \longrightarrow α) \operatorname{where} \operatorname{coe} d = d.\operatorname{toFun} @[\operatorname{simp}] \operatorname{theorem} \operatorname{equivariant\_apply} (d \colon \operatorname{Density} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) χ) (r \colon \operatorname{PosReal}) (u \colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{MetricBundle}) \colon d (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{dilateBundle} r u) = χ r \cdot d u = d.\operatorname{equivariant} r u @[\operatorname{ext}] \operatorname{theorem} \operatorname{ext} {d₁ d₂ \colon \operatorname{Equivariant}.\operatorname{Density} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) χ} (h \colon \forall u \colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{MetricBundle}, d₁.\operatorname{toFun} u = d₂.\operatorname{toFun} u) \colon d₁ = d₂ = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{cases} d₁ \operatorname{with} | \operatorname{mk} f₁ h₁ => \operatorname{cases} d₂ \operatorname{with} | \operatorname{mk} f₂ h₂ => \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hf} \colon f₁ = f₂ = \operatorname{funext} h \operatorname{cases} \operatorname{hf} \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hh} \colon h₁ = h₂ = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{apply} \operatorname{Subsingleton}.\operatorname{elim} \operatorname{cases} \operatorname{hh} \operatorname{rfl}\end{aligned}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Changing the reference scale changes the scale factor by the relative factor. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,s₁,s₂\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{Scale},\;x\colon M,\;g\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{MetricFiber} x,\quad \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{scaleFactor} s₁ g = \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{scaleFactor} s₂ g \cdot \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{relativeFactor} s₁ s₂ x
correspondence.leanType: theorem scaleFactor_eq_mul_relativeFactor (s₁ s₂ : Conf.Scale) {x : M} (g : Conf.MetricFiber x) : Conf.scaleFactor s₁ g = Conf.scaleFactor s₂ g * Conf.relativeFactor s₁ s₂ x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Convert a scale-dependent density to an equivariant function, using a chosen reference scale.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def toEquivariantUsing (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) (s₀ : Conf.Scale) : Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The construction is independent of the chosen reference scale. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem toEquivariantUsing_independent (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) (s₀ s₁ : Conf.Scale) : toEquivariantUsing (Conf := Conf) d s₀ = toEquivariantUsing (Conf := Conf) d s₁

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Intrinsic conversion from scale-dependent to equivariant form. This uses a chosen global scale. In explicit terms, for d of type density conf : equals conf χ, its defining expression is to Equivariant Using Conf : equals Conf d Classical.choice ‹Nonempty Conf.Scale›.
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def toEquivariant (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) [Nonempty Conf.Scale] : Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For d of type Density Conf : equals Conf χ, s₀ of type Conf.Scale, x of type M, and g of type Conf.Metric Fiber x, to Equivariant Conf : equals Conf d ⟨x, g⟩ equals χ Conf.scale Factor s₀ g times d s₀ x.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,d\colon \operatorname{Density} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) χ,\;\operatorname{Nonempty} \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{Scale},\;s₀\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{Scale},\;x\colon M,\;g\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{MetricFiber} x,\quad \operatorname{toEquivariant} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) d ⟨x, g⟩ = χ (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{scaleFactor} s₀ g) \cdot d s₀ x
correspondence.leanType: theorem toEquivariant_apply (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) [Nonempty Conf.Scale] (s₀ : Conf.Scale) (x : M) (g : Conf.MetricFiber x) : toEquivariant (Conf := Conf) d ⟨x, g⟩ = χ (Conf.scaleFactor s₀ g) * d s₀ x

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Restrict an equivariant density to the section defined by a scale. In explicit terms, for d of type density conf : equals conf χ, its defining expression is val s x : equals d ⟨x, s.metric x⟩ compat : equals by intro s₁ s₂ funext x change d ⟨x, s₂.metric x⟩ equals χ Conf.relative Factor s₁ s₂ x times d ⟨x, s₁.metric x⟩ rw ← Conf.relative Factor spec s₁ s₂ x simpa using d.equivariant Conf.relative Factor s₁ s₂ x ⟨x, s₁.metric x⟩.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{toScaleDependent}(d)=\operatorname{val} s x = d ⟨x, s.\operatorname{metric} x⟩ \operatorname{compat} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{intro} s₁ s₂ \operatorname{funext} x \operatorname{change} d ⟨x, s₂.\operatorname{metric} x⟩ = χ (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{relativeFactor} s₁ s₂ x) \cdot d ⟨x, s₁.\operatorname{metric} x⟩ \operatorname{rw} [← \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{relativeFactor\_spec} s₁ s₂ x] \operatorname{simpa} \operatorname{using} d.\operatorname{equivariant} (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{relativeFactor} s₁ s₂ x) ⟨x, s₁.\operatorname{metric} x⟩
correspondence.leanType: def toScaleDependent (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) : ConformalStructure.Density (Conf := Conf) χ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Equivalence between the two descriptions of densities, once a global scale exists. In explicit terms, its defining expression is to Fun : equals the function sending d to Conformal Structure.Equivariant.Density.to Scale Dependent Conf : equals Conf χ : equals χ d inv Fun : equals the function sending d to Conformal Structure.Density.to Equivariant Conf : equals Conf χ : equals χ d left inv : equals by intro d simpa using Conformal Structure.Equivariant.Density.to Equivariant to Scale Dependent Conf : equals Conf χ : equals χ d right inv : equals by intro d ext s x simpa using Conformal Structure.Density.to Scale Dependent to Equivariant Conf : equals Conf χ : equals χ d s x.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{equivScaleDependentEquiv}=\operatorname{toFun} = d \longmapsto \operatorname{ConformalStructure}.\operatorname{Equivariant}.\operatorname{Density}.\operatorname{toScaleDependent} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) (χ = χ) d \operatorname{invFun} = d \longmapsto \operatorname{ConformalStructure}.\operatorname{Density}.\operatorname{toEquivariant} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) (χ = χ) d \operatorname{left\_inv} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{intro} d \operatorname{simpa} \operatorname{using} (\operatorname{ConformalStructure}.\operatorname{Equivariant}.\operatorname{Density}.\operatorname{toEquivariant\_toScaleDependent} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) (χ = χ) d) \operatorname{right\_inv} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{intro} d \operatorname{ext} s x \operatorname{simpa} \operatorname{using} (\operatorname{ConformalStructure}.\operatorname{Density}.\operatorname{toScaleDependent\_toEquivariant} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) (χ = χ) d s x)
correspondence.leanType: noncomputable def equivScaleDependentEquiv [Nonempty Conf.Scale] : ConformalStructure.Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ ≃ ConformalStructure.Density (Conf := Conf) χ

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: Verified means that Lean checks the propositions encoded in this file and their imported assumptions. It does not assert that the present formal statement exhausts every analytic hypothesis or convention in the cited literature.
